title: Shef instruction — freezer-stocking job runbook, Jan 2026
html:
TL;DR

  • A freezer-restocking + door-timing runbook Yahir wrote for a job at Shef (the home-cooked meal delivery marketplace). Brief, operational.
  • Daily steps:
    • Make sure freezers are stocked. Last thing you do every day.
    • Check freezers in the morning — magnet changes.
    • Clean ice packs when they return if dirty, put back in freezer.
    • Downtime tasks: clean return bags, leave in sorting room.
    • Pro doors open at 1:30 (both doors).
    • Left door open at 8:30–10.

Key signal

Yet another bridge-income job running concurrently with Rankett founder work in early 2026.

Why it matters in the graph

  • Adds shef to the bridge-income stack alongside the gym overnight shifts (Memory, 2025 09 23 how im scraping by) and the grandma care work and the NaviWell clinic role. Yahir is running multiple bridge jobs simultaneously while building Rankett.
  • Same runbook-form as 2025 09 08 logging health data naviwell and 2025 08 05 40 minute — Yahir consistently produces handoff-quality SOPs even for low-status jobs. Useful pattern: his bridge-job output style is itself founder-grade.
  • Provides additional context for the 2026 01 02 diddles call / 2026 01 13 rankett com / 2026 01 11 untitled cluster — Yahir was working the Shef job in the same week as the Diddles call and the Rankett-self-doubt note.

Open questions

  • Is Shef a single-shift gig, recurring, or a contractor relationship?
  • Does Shef appear in Memory's bridge-income explicitly? (Currently Memory mentions "overnight shifts at a gym" only.)
